2026-04-09T09:01:02.9291023Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-09T09:01:02.9291462Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-09T09:01:02.9291844Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-09T09:01:02.9292729Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. It includes resource types such as `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, and `AdvancedThreatProtection`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is completely empty** — no code snippets demonstrating how to create a MySQL Flexible Server or related resources\\n- **No sample test file exists** — `tests/Samples/` directory has no `.cs` files; there are no snippet-backed code examples\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section contains only one generic sentence** — no description of the specific resource types available or how they relate to each other\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no links to related resources or documentation\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Examples` section with a code snippet placeholder, and fill in the empty sections. Follow the pattern from `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/README.md` as a reference.\\n\\nReplace the current README content with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET\\n\\nAzure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET.\\n\\n## Getting started\\n\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease\\n```\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You must have an [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library provisions Azure infrastructure using the Azure Developer CLI (azd) or Azure Bicep. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling — no explicit client authentication code is required in the provisioning definition itself.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\nThe main resource types provided by this library are:\\n\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — Represents an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — Represents a database within a flexible server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — Manages firewall rules to control access to the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — Manages server configuration parameters\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — Configures Azure Active Directory administrators for the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** / **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`** — Manages server backups\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Manages scheduled maintenance windows\\n- **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Configures Advanced Threat Protection for the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`** — Manages private endpoint connections\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to create a MySQL Flexible Server with administrator login credentials.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n## Troubleshooting\\n\\n- File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).\\n- Check [previous questions]((stackoverflow.com/redacted) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.\\n\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the base provisioning library\\n- See the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deployment tooling\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\nFor details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing guide][cg].\\n\\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit ((cla.microsoft.com/redacted)).\\n\\nWhen you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the bot. You\\u0026#39;ll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA.\\n\\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact (opencode@microsoft.com) with any other questions or comments.\\n\\n\\n[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md\\n[coc]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n[coc_faq]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a test demonstrating how to create a basic MySQL Flexible Server. Use `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/tests/Samples/` as a reference if it exists, otherwise model after `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`.\\n\\nThe test file should:\\n- Be in namespace `Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests`\\n- Use `[Test]` attribute (NUnit)\\n- Wrap the snippet content with `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` / `#endregion`\\n- Demonstrate creating an `Infrastructure`, adding parameters (admin login, admin password), creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` with `MySqlFlexibleServerSku`, `MySqlFlexibleServerVersion`, `MySqlFlexibleServerStorage`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties`, and `MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability`, then adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n\\nExample structure for `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.Primitives;\\nusing Azure.Provisioning.MySql;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests;\\n\\npublic class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    [Category(\\u0026#34;Samples\\u0026#34;)]\\n    public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic\\n        Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter adminLogin =\\n            new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string))\\n            {\\n                Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator username of the server.\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n        infra.Add(adminLogin);\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter adminPass =\\n          };\\n        infra.Add(adminPass);\\n\\n        MySqlFlexibleServer server =\\n            new(nameof(server))\\n            {\\n                Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default,\\n                Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n                AdministratorLogin = adminLogin,\\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass,\\n                Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\\n                {\\n                    StorageSizeInGB = 20\\n                },\\n                Backup = new It includes resource types such as `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, and `AdvancedThreatProtection`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is completely empty** — no code snippets demonstrating how to create a MySQL Flexible Server or related resources\\n- **No sample test file exists** — `tests/Samples/` directory has no `.cs` files; there are no snippet-backed code examples\\n- **\\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section contains only one generic sentence** — no description of the specific resource types available or how they relate to each other\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — no links to related resources or documentation\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the empty `## Examples` section with a code snippet placeholder, and fill in the empty sections. Follow the pattern from `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/README.md` as a reference.\\n\\nReplace the current README content with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET\\n\\nAzure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET.\\n\\n## Getting started\\n\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease\\n```\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You must have an [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library provisions Azure infrastructure using the Azure Developer CLI (azd) or Azure Bicep. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling — no explicit client authentication code is required in the provisioning definition itself.\\n\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\nThe main resource types provided by this library are:\\n\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — Represents an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — Represents a database within a flexible server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — Manages firewall rules to control access to the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — Manages server configuration parameters\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — Configures Azure Active Directory administrators for the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** / **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`** — Manages server backups\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Manages scheduled maintenance windows\\n- **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Configures Advanced Threat Protection for the server\\n- **`MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`** — Manages private endpoint connections\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to create a MySQL Flexible Server with administrator login credentials.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n## Troubleshooting\\n\\n- File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).\\n- Check [previous questions]((stackoverflow.com/redacted) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.\\n\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the base provisioning library\\n- See the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deployment tooling\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\nFor details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing guide][cg].\\n\\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. You\\u0026#39;ll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA.\\n\\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact (opencode@microsoft.com) with any other questions or comments.\\n\\n\\n[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md\\n[coc]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n[coc_faq]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a test demonstrating how to create a basic MySQL Flexible Server. Use `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/tests/Samples/` as a reference if it exists, otherwise model after `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`.\\n\\nThe test file should:\\n- Be in namespace `Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests`\\n- Use `[Test]` attribute (NUnit)\\n- Wrap the snippet content with `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` / `#endregion`\\n- Demonstrate creating an `Infrastructure`, adding parameters (admin login, admin password), creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` with `MySqlFlexibleServerSku`, `MySqlFlexibleServerVersion`, `MySqlFlexibleServerStorage`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties`, and `MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability`, then adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n\\nExample structure for `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.   {\\n                Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator username of the server.\\u0026#34;\\n            };\\n        infra.Add(adminLogin);\\n\\n        ProvisioningParameter adminPass =\\n            new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string))\\n            {\\n                Description = \\u0026#34;The administrator password of the server.\\u0026#34;,\\n                IsSecure = true\\n            };\\n        infra.Add(adminPass);\\n\\n        MySqlFlexibleServer server =\\n            new(nameof(server))\\n            {\\n                Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n                {\\n                    Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D2ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n                    Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose\\n                },\\n                CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default,\\n                Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021,\\n                AdministratorLogin = adminLogin,\\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass,\\n                Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage\\n                {\\n                    StorageSizeInGB = 20\\n                },\\n                Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties\\n                {\\n                    BackupRetentionDays = 7,\\n                    GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled\\n
